ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cinemas" (
  "cinema_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cinema */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the cinema is located */,
  "cinema_name" TEXT /* Name of the cinema */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for customer inquiries */,
  "address" TEXT /* Detailed address of the cinema */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude for mapping and location services */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude for mapping and location services */,
  "opening_hours" TEXT /* Operating hours of the cinema */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for official communications */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the cinema's official website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cinema_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language in which the movie is available */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the movie */,
  "director" TEXT /* Name of the movie director */,
  "producer" TEXT /* Name of the movie producer */,
  "cast" TEXT /* Main cast of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date of the movie's release */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country where the movie was produced */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Age rating of the movie (e.g., PG, R) */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie trailer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "showtimes" (
  "showtime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each showtime */,
  "cinema_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cinema where the movie is screened */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie being screened */,
  "showtime" TEXT /* Showtime of the movie */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the screening (e.g., Upper Balcony, Gold, Silver) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the showtime */,
  "screen_number" INTEGER /* Number of the screen where the movie is shown */,
  "total_seats" INTEGER /* Total number of seats available in the screen */,
  "available_seats" INTEGER /* Number of seats still available */,
  "is_3d" INTEGER /* Indicates if the show is in 3D */,
  "is_4dx" INTEGER /* Indicates if the show is in 4DX */,
  "is_vip" INTEGER /* Indicates if the show has VIP seating */,
  "is_soon" INTEGER /* Indicates if the show is a pre-release screening */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("showtime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_showtimes_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_showtimes_cinema_id FOREIGN KEY ("cinema_id") REFERENCES cinemas ("cinema_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ticket entry */,
  "showtime_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the showtime this ticket is linked to */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the ticket */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total seating capacity for the showtime */,
  "booked" INTEGER /* Number of tickets already booked */,
  "seat_number" TEXT /* Specific seat number (e.g., A1, B2) */,
  "row_number" TEXT /* Row number of the seat */,
  "seat_type" TEXT /* Type of seat (e.g., Regular, Premium, VIP) */,
  "booking_status" TEXT /* Status of the ticket (e.g., Available, Booked, Cancelled) */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., Paid, Unpaid) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_showtime_id FOREIGN KEY ("showtime_id") REFERENCES showtimes ("showtime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number for customer communication */,
  "address" TEXT /* Billing or mailing address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "membership_status" TEXT /* Status of the customer's membership (e.g., None, Silver, Gold) */,
  "points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points earned by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bookings" (
  "booking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each booking */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer who made the booking */,
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the ticket booked */,
  "booking_date" TEXT /* Date when the booking was made */,
  "number_of_tickets" INTEGER /* Number of tickets booked in this transaction */,
  "total_cost" REAL /* Total cost of the booking */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., Credit Card, PayPal) */,
  "booking_reference" TEXT /* Unique reference number for the booking */,
  "booking_status" TEXT /* Status of the booking (e.g., Confirmed, Pending, Cancelled) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("booking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_ticket_id FOREIGN KEY ("ticket_id") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for login */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, staff, customer) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity performed (e.g., login, booking) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the activity was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the activity performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
